input:
[SYNTHETIC] META: data_source_mode=synthetic; classification=SYNTHETIC-FICTIONAL; case_id=P2REQ-008
[SYNTHETIC] EVIDENCE REF: eval:P2REQ-008
[SYNTHETIC] SOURCE: JiraBoard | Avery Stone, Tool Steward | synthetic-2026-07-09T12:00:00Z | SYNTHETIC-FICTIONAL
[SYNTHETIC] SOURCE: ConfluenceNotes | Avery Stone, Tool Steward | synthetic-2026-07-09T12:10:00Z | SYNTHETIC-FICTIONAL
[SYNTHETIC] SOURCE: TeamsDigest | Avery Stone, Tool Steward | synthetic-2026-07-09T11:30:00Z | SYNTHETIC-FICTIONAL
[SYNTHETIC] STAKEHOLDER: Avery Stone, Tool Steward; Tool Steward
[SYNTHETIC] FACT: SignalBridge is the fictional tool-origin evidence case for this source-metadata slice.
[SYNTHETIC] FACT: JiraBoard issue snapshot says approval queue should include escalated requests.
[SYNTHETIC] FACT: ConfluenceNotes page says approval queue should exclude escalated requests until policy review.
[SYNTHETIC] FACT: TeamsDigest summary is older than the JiraBoard and ConfluenceNotes snapshots.
[SYNTHETIC] ASSUMPTION: The review stays draft/advisory and no source is treated as authoritative automatically.
[SYNTHETIC] INFERRED: Source staleness may affect which evidence should guide the next review.
[SYNTHETIC] CONFLICT: JiraBoard says escalated requests are included, while ConfluenceNotes says escalated requests are excluded until policy review.
[SYNTHETIC] QUESTION: Which source is authoritative for escalated-request queue behavior?
[SYNTHETIC] QUESTION: What source freshness rule should govern tool-origin evidence?

mock_responses.text: [SYNTHETIC] synthetic JiraBoard, ConfluenceNotes, and TeamsDigest metadata only

expected_output_characteristics.business_problem: [SYNTHETIC] SignalBridge has tool-origin evidence from JiraBoard, ConfluenceNotes, and TeamsDigest with conflicting escalated-request guidance and different source timestamps.
expected_output_characteristics.business_objective: [SYNTHETIC] Produce a draft advisory tool-origin evidence summary that preserves source metadata, conflict, staleness, classification, and evidence refs.
expected_output_characteristics.draft_requirement: [SYNTHETIC] Draft requirement: SignalBridge should preserve source system, owner, timestamp, retrieved timestamp, classification, conflict, and authoritative-source questions before any queue behavior is decided.
expected_output_characteristics.draft_story: [SYNTHETIC] As a synthetic Tool Steward, I want tool-origin evidence preserved with source metadata so that reviewers can decide which source governs escalated-request queue behavior.
facts: [SYNTHETIC] JiraBoard issue snapshot says approval queue should include escalated requests.
[SYNTHETIC] ConfluenceNotes page says approval queue should exclude escalated requests until policy review.
[SYNTHETIC] TeamsDigest summary is older than the JiraBoard and ConfluenceNotes snapshots.
assumptions: [SYNTHETIC] The review stays draft/advisory and no source is treated as authoritative automatically.
inferred_items: [SYNTHETIC] Source staleness may affect which evidence should guide the next review.
open_questions: [SYNTHETIC] Which source is authoritative for escalated-request queue behavior?
[SYNTHETIC] What source freshness rule should govern tool-origin evidence?

facts: [SYNTHETIC] ConfluenceNotes excludes escalated requests until policy review.
conflicts: [SYNTHETIC] JiraBoard queue behavior conflicts with ConfluenceNotes queue behavior.
